review_text: Very knowledgeable with estate planning. Jeremy was wonderful at answering all of our many questions in a way that was easily understood. He took the time to explain all of our choices to insure we took the decisions that best suited our needs. Jeremy also explained all of the final documentation so we were clear on any next steps.
Although I’m not a client, Jeremy upholds the most professionalism and kindness. I highly recommend him and his firm. His Legal Assistant, Traci, is one in a million and will work hard for you.

I had a great experience working with this firm on my estate plan. They were knowledgeable, patient, and explained everything in plain language. I felt heard, supported, and confident in the decisions I was making. Highly recommended for anyone who wants an organized, trustworthy, and responsive estate-planning attorney.
Mr. Ramsey is a very competent attorney who knows his way around with regards to organizing trusts and,wills, and other estate planning needs! Thankful to have been able to make his acquaintance.
owner_response: Thank you very much Abe! It was a blessing to be able to help you in this important step.

review_text: Jeremy took a daunting task and broke it down into manageable parts. It was truly a lovely experience doing our estate planning with Jeremy. He is friendly, patient, and incredibly organized. Thank you so much for everything and helping us protect our family and prepare for the future!
owner_response: Thank you very much for such kind words. It was an honor to help your family.

review_text: Jeremy helped my husband and I get a trust in m place so our finances were secure. It was very thorough so our loved ones could avoid costs and time not having to deal with probate.
owner_response: Thank you!

review_text: Jeremy has helped me with a few different things a special needs trust, my will, and also real estate transactions and investments, very knowledgeable in so many different fields. I am very grateful and satisfied for his dedication and help…
owner_response: Thank you!

review_text: Attorney Ramsey did a great professional job on our living trust and also on my mom’s living trust. Thanks for the great work!
owner_response: Thank you Lance!

review_text: "I'd like a range for a typical for sale by owner with no variable unknown costs considered so I know the baseline" "My rate is $325 an hr, I don't have a way to estimate the typical baseline" So we have no way of knowing if it'll be $2k or $10k.
owner_response: When you called regarding your for sale by owner real estate project, I advised that since real estate matters can be unpredictable, I could not quote an exact fee, which is true. I provided my hourly rate and a range of what the fees could be. Thank you for your consideration.

review_text: I could not even inquire about a Will or Power of Attorney without being judged for having a phone number area code that was not 815. I had exact opposite experience of most reviews here and could not get a word in from this person. If you don't have an 815 number don't waste your time. This was a disappointing referral. I chose to find a Rockford attorney with a more professional, kinder personality.
owner_response: I have clients all throughout the states of Wisconsin and Illinois. Many of my clients are in the Rockford area because they would like to meet with me in person about the important subject of estate planning. I asked about your area code to make sure if you would be comfortable working with me at a distance, which you were not. To suggest that I would turn down a client because of their area code is absurd.

review_text: Jeremy is dedicated and very thorough using his extensive knowledge to help people with their estate planning and other legal needs. He very adeptly helps people in this crucial area of their lives.
Mr. Ramsey is a tough, thorough and easy to understand attorney! We appreciate the help he provided us with.

My husband and I worked with Jeremy for our estate planning. He is incredibly knowledgeable, experienced, and thorough, bringing a high level of integrity to what he does. I expected the process of creating a will and thinking through everything to be daunting, however Jeremy ensured that we covered it all and guided us through our different options, patiently answering all of our questions from the beginning. He was very kind and made the process painless. Would highly recommend.

Kevin Frost was outstanding in handling my son's personal injury case. After his car accident, Kevin helped him understand his options. Kevin also went above and beyond to ensure he receive the best medical attention while he handled the legalities. He is a skilled negotiator and a dedicated advocate which resulted in a significant settlement. I am grateful for his service and highly recommend him to anyone without any reservations.
owner_response: Thank you, Paul, for sharing your thoughtful review! We’re so glad to hear that Kevin was able to support your son after his car accident and secure a great outcome. Our team is always here to help families who need a personal injury lawyer in the Rockford, IL area. If you or someone you know ever needs trusted legal guidance, don’t hesitate to reach out.

review_text: Kevin Frost is a very thorough and competent personal injury lawyer. Kevin always had my son’s best interests in mind. He kept us informed of the legal process and what to expect from the insurance company. Kevin took a stressful situation and made everything an easy process. Kevin’s commitment to my son through his integrity, diligence, knowledge of the law and his persistence exceeded our expectations. We highly recommend Kevin Frost!
owner_response: Thank you so much, Claire! We’re truly grateful for your kind words and are glad we were able to make the process smooth and reassuring for your family. If anyone you know ever needs a personal injury lawyer in Rockford, IL, our team at Clark Frost Zucchi is here to provide the same level of care and dedication. Don’t hesitate to reach out. We’re always ready to help.

review_text: Mr. Frost is wonderful to work with and we were very pleased with how everything was handled. He made the process so easy and was there to help and answer all of our questions. Very happy with how the matter turned out and definitely recommend him and his office.
owner_response: Thanks Darcie for the excellent rating. We are proud to be a top choice for Personal Injury cases in the greater Rockford region. It is our goal to provide the best outcome for our clients. Our team at Clark Frost Zucchi works diligently to make the process easy for our clients and keeping them informed along the way. Thanks again for your trust in our firm and Kevin Frost.

review_text: Didn't even get past the phone call to schedule an appointment. Whoever answered the phone was not pleasant or helpful. I immediately knew I would never recommend this law practice to anyone.

Just know that your "Free initial consultation" is NOT free. You will be charged $50 when you make your consultation appointment and this $50 is non-refundable, even if you decide not to hire them!! So you are paying them to have an appointment with them!! Unbelievable!! They claim it's for administration fees!! What a scam. Please save yourself the headache, find a different attorney.
A very simple probate has taken more then 2 years! Still waiting!!!
Late on getting back to u

I have been denied disability twice now. I called the firm for advice and help. After a series of about 4 to 5 questions, the woman on the phone told me that I was not going to be able to receive disability because I claim an income. My income is less than 12,000 a year. I was informed that you cannot claim any kind of income if you want to be approved for disability and she hung up on me.
owner_response: We sincerely apologize for your recent experience with our firm. We understand how challenging and disheartening it can be to navigate the complexities of disability claims, especially after facing denials. Regarding our policy, we must clarify that our firm typically does not take cases where the claimants are currently working. This policy stems from our historical data showing a significantly lower success rate in cases where clients have ongoing employment. Although the government allows part-time work while receiving Social Security benefits, our track record in these scenarios has not been favorable. We regret if this policy and its implications were not communicated to you empathetically and clearly. Our goal is always to provide the best possible guidance, even when we cannot take on a case. If you have further questions or need more detailed explanations, please don’t hesitate to contact Shawn, who will be more than willing to assist you. Again, we are sorry for any frustration or inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding of our position. Sincerely, Shawn Good The Good Law Group

review_text: I urge you not to use this group for they are a very crooked law office that plays game with the system at the expense of their clients. They were not honest. On the day of my hearing the most important date, Mr Good senior sends his son to represent me, his son makes me sign a new client form to allow him to represent me. Of course I sign with his son out of desperation because my hearing is in 10 minutes. His son Good jr acted very crass towards the judge and ultimately ended up threatening the judge by saying I don't care what decision you make we will appeal! I signed up with Mr Good senior, someone I thought was an honest experienced attorney and he completely let me down and sent his inexperienced son to represent me at my hearing last minute! Again go with the bigger groups you see on TV... this law group is no good!
owner_response: We regret to hear that you are displeased with your recent experience with us. We appreciate you bringing this experience to our attention. -Shawn

review_text: Call these guys if you want someone to tell you to do it yourself and they'll file the papers. Their phone assistant was hired to tell people that they should go on the SS website. Way to go. What you're saying is you won't help unless it's been figured out already. What if my disability doesn't allow me to do such things? Call elsewhere.
owner_response: We are sorry we could not help you. Without the information we need regarding eligibility for benefits there is nothing we can do. If you want to call me directly, please call my office at 847/577-4476 and ask for Shawn.

review_text: They do not return calls. After my 4th call to inquire status of my case the receptionist said she was told to tell me I was too old for them to represent me in a disability case.
owner_response: We are sorry you think we did not return your calls. We pride ourselves on being highly responsive so this feedback is troubling. Generally, if you are at or above full retirement age you are not eligible for benefits. This is not our rules, it is federal law. -Shawn

review_text: Shawn was very friendly and also very helpful in getting me to and from the court hearing. In my current situation, I would have not had been able to get there otherwise.

If you want to pay someone $3,500 just to be screamed at and thrown out of their office, then this is the lawyer for you. I hired him to handle my case, but during what should have been a routine office visit, he lost his temper simply because I was wearing a beer T-shirt under a zipped hoodie. When I calmly told him I didn’t appreciate being spoken to that way—especially after paying him thousands of dollars—he threw my folder across the desk, told me it was “16 hours of work” and that I wouldn’t be getting anything back, then ordered his secretary to kick me out. I never received a refund, never got proper legal help from him, and ultimately had to hire another attorney (Albert Altamore) to fix the mess. This incident happened about a year ago, and I waited until all my court matters were resolved before posting this review to make sure he couldn’t jeopardize my case. I’m writing this now to warn others: save your money, time, and peace of mind.
owner_response: Adrianna, thank you for your feedback. The attorney that handled your case is no longer with the firm. We strive to provide only the best representation for all of our clients. If the need ever arises, we hope you would you would consider contacting us for a free consultation.

review_text: Divorces are messy even if you don’t want them to be! Having a paralegal like Maria makes a huge difference. She GETS it! And I knew she was in my corner all the way! Mason was great and he could not do what he does without a team behind him. It was an annoying, frustrating process but the Crosby team did what they could within the confines of the law. Unfortunately, laws can hamstring alot of things but Mason and Maira did a good job of guiding where it matters most. Thanks to you both!

The process of my divorce took 5 months. You will mostly be working with the paralegals which are not friendly at all. It’s an inconvenience if you call or email. Don’t expect fast responses. Absolutely no compassion either. Divorce is a difficult process but you are on your own. I had to remind them of all the demands I had. They even missed a few demands that I had required. I felt like an inconvenience to them. Mason & Tyler are kind. But they need better paralegals. I’m just glad this is over with.

If I could give 6 stars, I would. I made a terrible mistake and was charged with a DUI. I’ve never been in trouble with the law, not even a ticket, so my anxiety was eating me alive. I came across Paul and, after reading all of the reviews, I chose him to represent me in court, and it was probably one of the best decisions I made. Not only did he reassure me when it came to anxiety, but he was hands-on every step of the way. Every time I had a question or concern about anything, he responded quickly, whether it was through text or call. He fought for me until the very end and got me the best possible outcome. He was able to get my license suspension lifted for me with having to take a few treatment classes, court supervision, and pay fines, so if you’re ever in need of an attorney, whatever the case may be, Paul is your guy to go to. Thank you again, Paul, you’re the best!

I had a great experience with The Law Office of Paul M. Marriett. They handled my Rockford CDL ticket efficiently and kept me informed throughout the entire process. No stress, no confusion, and everything was resolved just like they said it would be. Professional, reliable, and worth it. I would definitely recommend them to anyone needing representation.

The attorney took on my case, worked it for over a month then “realized “ he couldn’t anymore bc the arresting officer was his cousin. Not sure how he didn’t know this before because he had the police report, but then he charged me the full amount and I was stuck without legal council

Had a similar experience to the other 1 start review. Called for a consult/information. Was forwarded to one of the attorneys from the front desk. Not sure the gentleman’s name, but right of the rip he was extremely rude and condescending. Barely gave me any opportunity to speak or explain my situation, only heard what he wanted to hear and then would cut me off and start rambling. Almost feel like this gentleman just enjoyed hearing his own voice. Will never attempt to go through this lawfirm again and would not recommend anybody else to try them. The biggest kick is that this gentleman casually threw in my face that the amount of money I was seeking legal action for was basically chump change and not worth his time. Well not everybody makes big lawyer money like you my friend, for some people that amount of money could genuinely be life changing.
Pros: Ryan Varner is a pretty good guy. Cons: The office staff is completely inept at their job.

I have great reservations about not awarding the firm five stars. Thus far, the representation has been outstanding. The lawyers are caring, intelligent, know the law, and are aggressive in representing my interests, and I am sure, the interests of all there clients. That the litigation is more is expensive than I, and I am sure they, anticipated is because the circumstances so unusual that they aren't likely to happen again anytime soon and it would have happened whatever law firm I retained. That said, trying to deal with the billing department is a nightmare. That is not to say that any of the enumerated costs in the billing statements I have received are out of the ordinary--if anything certain things appear to be billed somewhat conservatively: in my experience, O'Flaherty law compares favorably with the standard rates for much smaller firms that do not have nearly the same resources and experience. However, dealing with the billing department is a nightmare. Once, I thought a retainer had been paid, and months later I was notified it was not and that representation would be put on hold. Sometimes, a retainer refill will be $5,000, sometimes $10,000, and the discrepancy is never explained. Very shortly after paying the 10,000 I got a letter saying "your retainer has been depleted and is insufficient to cover a past-due balance," and asked for $5,0000. I was shocked, asked about it, and was told that it had not been depleted, that the 5,000 was essentially to get the trust back up to 10,000 but that the matter had been placed on hold until this was paid. Yet the due date to pay it was seven days later making me wonder if the matter had been placed on hold or would be placed on hold in seven days. (Aren't lawyers supposed to be precise about these things?) Nor does it seem had my lawyer paid any heed (he was more worried about my case than the billing department and there was still money in the trust.) and a couple of weeks later while considering how I would pay the retainer, I got a past due balance for a much smaller amount (under 2,000 dollars), but nowhere did it say the matter would be placed on hold until the outstanding balance was paid which perhaps would have been the most sensible place to take such action. Aren't lawyers supposed to be precise with language? Again, as far as I can tell there is nothing enumerated in the invoices that I can see which isn't relevant, necessary, or prudent in regards to the litigation, and when enumerated is billed at a very reasonable rate for a lawyer. The problem is that what the billing department says doesn't make any sense and when you ask for clarification, they respond with "oh what it says isn't true." Also, the enumerated hours come in the mail, and unsurprisingly will come at completely different time then email requests for a retainer to be refilled or a past due balance to be paid. It might be helpful it there were a secure online portal where one could examine the amount paid, the expenses billed, the retainer, any unpaid balances. I also asked the billing department how much the case had cost in total, but I didn't receive a response. (I've asked again, and I'm sure I will.) This is not over-billing or anything sinister, but it is a mess.

I had a consultation with Mr. Oancea regarding a business Small Claims Court case in Cook County, and I’m very grateful for his guidance. He carefully reviewed our case, explained the process step by step, and clearly outlined what we could realistically expect. What stood out most was his honesty — he did not make unrealistic promises, but instead gave straightforward, practical advice based on experience. I truly felt that he cared and wanted to help. I highly recommend Iurie Oancea as an attorney in Cook County. Thank you for your time and professionalism. I truly appreciate your honesty, guidance, and support.

I reached out to the attorney for a few legal clarifications and I was very satisfied with the service. He answered all of my questions patiently and provided the necessary information with clarity and professionalism. I truly appreciated his helpful attitude and clear explanations. I would definitely recommend his services to anyone who needs reliable legal advice
I really appreciate both attorneys they both did an amazing job on my husband’s case. He’s home and we thank both of them. Alex is a beast, the Judge agreed with everything he said.

These guys are horrible. First court date the Lawyer showed up late didn’t even know he had to come. Second court date came around another lawyer showed up and all she did was fix the other lawyers promblems that he had done. They sent me go another courtroom I wasn’t suppose to go to. The lawyers were extremely rude and one actually racist saying that “ oh I deal with your kind all the time” like seriously?! This was my first time having to get a lawyer and had to deal with these scums. Nothing but wasting my time and money. They call themselves lawyers but they are just idiot with certificates.

Where do I start with this review. We were referred to McNamee & Maheney Ltd. by a close friend and contacted them right away, gave our deposit and we never heard from them for over a year. We tried and tried to contact the office and always got told that our message was left for our lawyer, come to find out he did not even work there anymore and our case was forgotten about. We tried for another couple months to get answers and got nothing. We finally got an appointment, when we showed up they said the lawyer was on vacation and we still could not get our deposit back, and they would send a check in the mail. Two weeks went by and I called to see if it was in the mail, and they had not sent it and would put in directly in our account, finally three days later it was there. So bottom line if you want to get ignored and forgot about go with McNamee & Mahoney, but save your money and find another lawyer!
Professional, likeable, accessible and honest
Don't know the outcome yet

We submitted all of the paperwork for our living will and trust and after six weeks, nothing was completed at all. After following up a month and one half later, we found that nothing had been worked on. When we questioned the status, we were told that “we never gave a timeframe”. We ended up going with a different provider.

It was a fantastic experience working with the Baudin Law Group!! They are like family and still feel that way. Randy , Kelly and Myrna you guys are the best! We appreciate everything you guys did for our family.
owner_response: Al, thank you so much for the kind words! It was a true privilege to stand by you and your loved ones; we really do consider you all a part of our family here. We never take for granted the trust you placed in us to help navigate your case. We hope you all have a wonderful holiday season and that 2026 is your best year yet! If you ever need anything at all, please don’t hesitate to reach out. Wishing you the best, Randy, Kelly, Myrna, and the rest of the staff at the Baudin Law Group, Ltd.

review_text: I hired Dane Loizzo as my third attorney during my divorce case. Within just three weeks, Dane racked up over $7,000 in charges, many of which appeared inflated, before withdrawing from my case without achieving any meaningful progress. Dane’s father, Tom Loizzo, is a well-regarded attorney with a strong reputation, and my mother has worked with Tom for decades. However, my experience with Dane was entirely different. He failed to provide value, transparency, or support. Based on my experience, I would not recommend Dane Loizzo for any divorce-related matters.

I was billed for a consultation that I never knew wasn’t free! I was made aware of the per hour rate but not that the consultation was being billed! The attorney I spoke with was not very knowledgeable and I came out of the meeting without really knowing much more that what I had already googled. Then they charged me $250!? I would be concerned what else they might charge for and not disclose. The other attorney in Burlington I met with was a FREE consultation and they actually provided me with additional information I never would have thought about! Go with the “other” attorney in Burlington not these people.
owner_response: We are sorry for any confusion, Theresa, but you were informed of the fee before scheduling and during the consultation. At no time were you told the consultation would be free.

review_text: Pleasure to work with

My husband and I both sold and purchased a home simultaneously. We could not have navigated through the legal side of things without the help of Shannon Wynn and staff, at Wynn at Law, LLC. Wynn at Law was with us every step of the way, with ease and genuine concern. They were always available for any questions/concerns we had and they responded very quickly. We recommend Wynn at Law, for expertise, vast legal knowledge, and for a commitment to their clientele that is rare to find today.
owner_response: Thank you for trusting us with such an important transition. Selling and buying at the same time can feel overwhelming, and I’m glad we could make the legal side feel manageable and straightforward. It was truly a pleasure working with you, and we’re grateful for the trust you placed in Wynn at Law, LLC. Wishing you all the best in your new home — and please feel free to reach out anytime if you need us.

review_text: Very professional atmosphere if you need a lawyer
Got the job done and won so to speak
Very polite staff! Would recommend!

I don't know who left a 1 star for this guy! I had to retain Scott several years ago and recently referred someone to him because he will actually give you his time for FREE over the phone! Scott will give you the best case scenario and worst case scenario without taking your money! I myself and people I have referred have never actually had an attorney get on the phone and lay it all out. Thanks Scott for being a good, honest, attorney!
Friendly staff. Closed our house on here.
Good advice
Did not totally procure all evdence related to my case!

4.5 Star Rating! The stars above did not give me that option. The short story is I hired Attorney Mehlos to help me fight a battery charge. The real story is Corey helped me through the worst year of my life. The short: I was being attacked and called the police. When they arrived I did not want the "attacker" arrested, because it was really just an argument that went to far and I was not injured. That being the case I did not say anything other than "Thanks for coming. Everything is okay". Unfortunately the other party was very mad and completely lied to the police and I was arrested. I was innocent and it was frustrating because they also too things that were said completely out of context. From day 1, Attorney Mehlos and I came up with a plan and continued to refine our strategy until the prosecutor dismissed the battery charge, before we were to go to trial. It took 12 months to get it done and it nearly killed me. Obviously there is a lot more to it, but I am not writting a book! LOL There was a lot of back and forth with the Prosecutor Assistant D.A. and the Judges. It was completely overwhelming at times to be an innocent man who has never been convicted of any crime and yet it seemed that everyone except Attorney Mehlos assumed I was guilty. It was comforting to know that Attorney Mehlos was confident and prepared to win at trial. The prosecutor eventually released knowing he’d have a tough case. Yet on multiple occasions Attorney Mehlos told me to trust him and the result was exactly what he predicted. I highly recommend Attorney Mehlos for any criminal case. Sincerely, Jeff R. Brownell Owner & Operator TaskMasters Home Services LLC

In January of 2023, after a 26 year untarnished and successful career as a firefighter/paramedic and 12 years as an adjunct fire instructor, I was falsely accused, charged and arrested for 5 different counts which consisted of both felonies and misdemeanors ranging from 4th degree sexual assault of a child to misconduct in office. During the 5 days I spent in jail, my wife received a recommendation to call attorney Corey Mehlos. That decision turned out to be the one that saved my life both literally and figuratively. The passion, professionalism, determination, dedication and commitment not just from Corey, but his entire staff is second to none. He never gave up on me or my family and after nearly 3 long years, his tireless work ended with an absolute domination in the court room. I was found not guilty and cleared overwhelmingly on all charges. This man is the absolute definition of what a defense attorney should be. He is truly fearless, and I wouldn’t hesitate to recommend him to anyone and every one who finds themself in need of legal assistance. Casper Mehlos law firm is THE REAL DEAL
owner_response: John; Thank you very much for your review! We could not be more happy that we were able to work with you to completely exonerate you of all charges at your jury trial this last week! We are sorry that you had to go through a three year nightmare of being falsely accused with such serious crimes the jury unanimously found you Not Guilty of committing. We appreciate your positive feedback for Attorney Mehlos and our team. It's been a pleasure getting to know you and your family. We look forward to staying in touch and doing whatever we can to help you as you move forward with your life. Thank you for trusting us and don't hesitate to let us know if you need anything. Stay in touch and all the best! --The Team at Casper Mehlos Law Group

review_text:
I truly wonder if other Black people had great experiences here. They accepted thousands of dollars but never really did anything towards my son's appeal, never really fought. In the beginning, one of the assistant attorneys (maybe even a paralegal, not too sure) that worked the case seemed to be good for a quick sec, but then was transferred or something. The case was transferred to one of the law firm attorneys. I believed he emailed me once, never really talked to me or updated me much. Then the case was given to a younger lady whom handled it until the decision. They never even really had meetings with me or my son. Truly horrible, and adds to how certain persons are treated in this business.

I read the other reviews on how this firm worked with those persons, my family and our case received none of this. I don't even think I ever met with anyone other than the paralegal but we spent around 15-20 grand. Truly a disappointing experience.
No one ever returns a call..found another lawyer who has

I’m disappointed in the service provided by Fields Law firm. My son’s appeal was denied and I understand that is how insurance companies work but it was sent directly to the disability provider without our being able to review. Follow-up discussion included a recommendation to go back to settle and waive benefits for life. However, most recently I sent them an email and no response. Then out of the blue they send me an email to which I replied and no response. I’m left wondering if the attorney on our case is even still employed there.

I wish I could write a positive review, but unfortunately, I cannot. As I told my lawyer several times, I was not happy with the outcome. I feel my lawyer was representing the defendant instead of me. It seemed that my lawyer was not trying to get me to the best outcome. I spent hours building spreadsheets explaining the way the defendant cost me lifetime money, but my lawyer seemed to ignore my concerns. It was my lawyer who talked me out of going to court before a judge. The final decision was mine based on my lawyer telling to accept the "final offer".

I would not recommend this law firm.

Well I don’t know why I can’t seem to get responses back in a “respectful client compassionate way” from Aliya the paralegal for Annie Surbaugh. I send email after email without responses by email back or phone calls. Then my last email 11/18/2022 to Annie Surbaugh comes back that she is out of the office until 11/28/2022. Now I must wait 10 days more for a response back because apparently Aliya Larson(paralegal) is out too?? Seriously a law firm can close for 10 days no warning to clients by email who are waiting for responses? VERY UNPROFESSIONAL and I will be happy when I am done with this firm!

I have had to do all the legwork of my disability appeal since original submission in December. Crickets from my legal aide. Talked to her once and she had no idea what was going on. When my great rep, Danielle, left I was also left out. A shame too. I had high hopes :-(

My LTD appeal took a long time. I believe Fields Law did not do anything I could not have done on my own  I was still very involved with collecting medical  records and following up with my doctors.  I do have a complicated medical history with 9 doctors at 3 large university hospitals . Medical Records delays were the reason for my LTD case to be closed resulting in an appeal. Ultimately the the appeal was approved. All things considered the fee seemed to be high for what they did.
Sued for 120k settlement was 20k not satisfied with their efforts

I was disappointed in the entire process. I was assigned to 3 different attorney's. Each time a little information and understanding of my case was lost. I feel my settlement amount suffered due to this issue. After 2 years, I received a final settlement offer. I was advised if I didn't accept it and was awarded benefits through the court process, the insurance company can stop paying benefits at any time or possibly not be awarded any benefits. Then the process of proving disability begins all over again... the thought of that vicious circle just wasn't worth it. The entire staff is really nicr,  professional & qualified.
I'm not sure if they have a large turnover issue resulting in my unfortunate situation. It would have been great if someone keep me in the loop. I kept calling & sending messages without replies. I finally I went through the main number and was told my attorney was no longer working there. I received a call after my case was reassigned to new attorney. He was well versed in my case and very positive. Then towards the end of the process, I received a call from the final attorney to tell me he was now handling my case. He was totally uninformed of the details of my case.

My original attorney felt I had a strong enough case to go all the way to litigation. She left the practice and my case was handed off to another attorney who was only interested in settling the case and getting it off his desk. He pushed for me to accept a settlement worth a third of what I was entitled to. I stupidly agreed. It is done and in the past now. Nothing more I can do.

I think there could have been more communication from lawyer at times I had to ask what’s going on with the case.
Lawyers assistant a lot of times did not respond to me especially regarding ambulance bill we should not have had to pay and was assured it would be taken care of instead was taken out of our taxes! Still not satisfied with that outcome.
At beginning of case I was told we were looking at a six figure settlement and as time went on I was lucky to get a lot less for being out of work for almost two years. I kind of felt mislead.
I felt at times that my case wasn’t important or a waste of time for the lawyer. I felt lawyer should have let me know that someone else was going to attend a second interview with opposing council.
With that said
Lawyer was very nice in person. This is my second time with this firm.

Fields law firm was really helpful. The legal team kept in close contact with me, from start to finish. They were very knowledgeable about disability claims in general,  and knew what to do through every step of my case. Having someone go through the long legal process of a disability case with you, like they did was invaluable.
owner_response: Glad to know that you had a great experience.

review_text:
I wasn’t planning to leave a review, but after today I’m done. I hired Hoglund & Mrozik, P.L.L.C. for a criminal matter. At first, everything seemed solid. Unfortunately, that didn’t last.

I was given guidance that ultimately cost me over $1,000 and wasn’t fully explained—especially how certain actions would affect me later. My case was also delayed nearly five months, only to end in a plea deal that likely could have happened much earlier.

I was prepared to let all of that go. What pushed me over the edge was being contacted six months after my case was settled and told I still owed money. Thankfully, I keep records. After I provided receipts, they reviewed it again and confirmed I had, in fact, paid in full.

Make of that what you will.

Paid $2400 cash to retain Robert Hoglund plus $348 filing fee just to end up in bankruptcy court to pay the trustee another $2500. I didn’t do my homework and suffered in the end. I initially called for a consultation and before I knew it I was signing papers. The staff (paralegals) Erica Olson, Elvin Rengifo, and Abbi Kutz are rude, don’t return calls, and only want to respond through emails at their convenience. I emailed Robert Hoglund not long before my court date and said how I was going to leave a review and he ended up sending Doug Weimerskirich to represent me after he assured me that he’d be in court with me. The entire process was a huge headache that was costly. When you retain them, they work for you, you don’t work for them. I somehow spent a lot of time sending in bank statements, check stubs, and other documents that I could have done had I filed on my own. I DO NOT RECOMMEND THIS LAW FIRM. Call around and get free consultations before you pay this company. YOU’VE BEEN WARNED.

If I could go after them for misrepresentation I definitely would. Shortly after becoming my attorney and having a court date there was a massive wait. I had to call the disability office in the cities and see what is going on because my attorney would always say "we are waiting for them to make a decision." What ended up happening is they were waiting for an income statement that wasn't coming for 6 months. I had to tell my attorney when our next court date was more than once because they wouldn't keep checking on my case. I also was on the phone with the judge's assistant trying to get things moving forward because my attorney was never checking on my case to try and move things forward. I was approved for disability but they stopped working when they got paid. I tried multiple times to speak to an attorney about my back pay but I was belittled by multiple attorneys saying "I should be grateful for them getting me approved." When I explained the situation they simply told me "everything looks and sounds correct." My back pay was incorrect and after a year of them getting phone calls from me asking for updates they said "nothing can be done and everything is correct as far as your back pay." I then called and asked to speak to the brand partner and left a message but I only received a letter from the brand partner saying "nothing can be done about your back pay" so I had to make a few calls. After one phone call I was able to get my back pay figured out and the disability office said that there was an error and they were going to correct it. If I didn't do the leg work that my attorney never did I wouldn't be receiving the correct amount because my attorney wasn't going to get paid anymore money because they already maxed out their pay. Stay away from Hoglund Law.

Would not recommend to anyone! SS helped me get out! This law firm did NOT turn in any medical records and is horrible with communication, went 2 1/2 years to lose all because my medical records were not turned in. SS reached out to me which never happens. I was so thankful, now I know what my next step is! This has not only hurt me but hurt my family. I truly believe this has been a money scam to them and they just truly don't care about their customers!!! I even offered to get my medical records and they said they had, but didn't!!! I hope and pray no one has to go through what my family has been put through.
Communication was bad, didn’t solve anything with my case, only wasted a year of my time and 2k. Still lost my license and had to pay 1500+ to the courts/DMV for a misdemeanor.

Called to get help for the person on the phone to get every little detail of my personal life and business for 45 mins to be emailed while on the phone saying they won't work with me. But won't explain why and give you the run around. Stay away from this place!

Lawyers and associate are not caring at all. Waited 2 years and only heard from them 2 or 3 times in the 2 years and only heard from the actual attorney a day before the hearing. I get that they are busy but they didn’t even get all my health information that they were supposed to. I don’t believe this is normal for an attorney. I had to call numerous times and they still haven’t returned my call after my case. They never answered the questions I had either or explained the case to me well enough considering my disability. Now I’m left with no way to survive without this support. Very frustrating, they are not helpful or professional. I don’t recommend this place.

Hoglund attorneys would not help because it wasn't worth the money they would collect. I would get more money (SSI) than retirement pay I'm receiving now. Don't let them fool you, they want only the big and quick (open and shut) cases that make the most profit! Secretary Candice will act like they're caring and can make your settlement quick and big and easy, as they ask every single fact in your life, including your entire social security number, which is unacceptable. I gave only the last four numbers and was ok to proceed. I had two letters from neurosurgeon and orthopedic doctors stating that I was 100% disabled. Still they wouldn't take the case. So come to your own conclusion, they are in it only for profit.

Horrible appointment set up, phone tag, and no one seemed to know I had an appointment set up. Had to repeat the same information many times to many different people only to find out they're not even licensed in my state. Absolute joke.

It’s crazy how someone’s life had been torn apart because of my injury, lost income, my potential has diminished, and the strain on my relationships. How bad does someone have to be to get any help? And because I can’t make enough now has created so much debt. I’m not a lazy person or looking for any handouts, but thanks for the support.

Completely disappointed – do not trust this firm with your disability case.

I am beyond frustrated and disappointed with this law firm, and I feel compelled to warn others. Not only did they fail me during my appeal, but they also handled my original Social Security disability application, which was denied. Now I’m left in an even worse situation than before, struggling to get the help I need.

After my initial denial, I reached out to the firm for assistance with the appeal, hoping they’d offer the expertise I needed. Instead, the firm canceled or didn’t show up for multiple calls after I informed them about the denial. It was like they didn’t care, and I was just left hanging.

Eventually, a paralegal named Juliana was supposed to follow up with me, but instead I got an outsourced contractor from Mexico who was completely unprepared and unfamiliar with my case. I have cognitive impairments, and the communication barrier made it impossible to get the correct information across. Not only could she not understand what I was saying, but she couldn’t even spell basic details correctly.

By the end of the call, it became clear that this person wasn’t offering any real legal advice—she was just filling out an appeal form on the Social Security website. This person had access to all of my information, including my social security number, and was working from her home in a small town in Mexico, which raised data security concerns. The attorney I hired has not spoken to me. They never reviewed my case, never offered insight into why my initial application was denied, and didn’t suggest any ways to strengthen the appeal. Just today I contacted Carrie, my case manager, to ask questions about an appointment that was scheduled by social security and she was unable to answer what the tests listed on the appointment sheet were.

This firm handled my original disability application, and I trusted them to do everything they could to help me. Instead, they’ve shown nothing but neglect and unprofessionalism. My family and I depend on these benefits to survive, and it feels like they’re not taking that seriously at all. The lack of communication, the failure to provide real legal counsel, and the overall disregard for my case is infuriating.

I would strongly advise anyone seeking Social Security disability assistance to avoid this firm at all costs. Save yourself the stress, time, and heartache.

This law firm has a concerning track record of turning down potential clients without conducting a proper evaluation of their cases. Specifically, they often do not take the time to thoroughly review medical records and pertinent documentation when asked to represent individuals facing social security issues. This apparent disregard for essential information not only undermines the clients' chances of receiving the assistance they need, but it also raises questions about the firm's commitment to providing comprehensive legal support.
Keri Dennis, one of the staff who returns calls, is rude and has zero compassion for clients and what they are going through. Complete attitude every call. I do not recommend this firm.

They have a review screening for checking to see what cases are easy wins and which require them to actually work. I was turned down based on a fraction of the total information. In my opinion this law firm cares about money and not their client. I would use caution when doing business here.

Over two years and still waiting!!! I have not received any information about my case. Whenever I call I get attitude. They have no answers for why it is taking this long. They have no knowledge about anything when I call to ask about my case. This has been 2+ years and still have not heard anything.
Waste of time. Had me on hold 20 minutes to tell me they couldn’t take my case, without even hearing what it was about.

Contacted Hoglund to help with disability case. They said they would. Took all my info and had me sign papers to retain them. I keep calling them to talk to my agent about my case. So far my assigned agent will never return my calls after several attempts.

Everyone involved with this law firm could use some sensitivity training. They have no idea how to deal with a client that has anxiety and brain issues that affect their memory and thought process. I will probably need another lawyer to get me out of my contract with them now since I no longer want to work with them. I didn't even get through 5 minutes of the intake process without getting completely frustrated and was nearly in tears due to the guy's lack of consideration for my issues that caused me to apply for disability benefits. I do not recommend this firm to anyone who has similar issues to mine.

Absolutely horrible. Matthew Harmoning was so rude over the phone. I had set up an appointment and speaking with administration said I have a piece of paper that I would like to show in person. I was told that most appointments were done over the phone and it wasn't necessary. It was also talked about me not missing work to come in. So I agreed to a phone appointment. Then I was rudely accused of not saying anything like that over the phone, blaming me for not coming in when that's what I wanted in the first place. Then said I couldn't listen to the recording. I would give zero stars if possible.

Worst experience I’ve had with a law firm. You can’t ever talk to an actual lawyer. A paralegal is assigned to your case. Keri Dennis was my case manager. I spoke with her a total of 2 times in 6 weeks because I had a few questions about the process. She audibly sighed for every question I asked and almost hung up on me during both conversations because she had more important things to do than answer my questions about the disability process and my case. Horrible experience.

Then you responded to my complaint by saying other people had good experiences? And I can call and talk to the rude person that tries to hang up on me during our conversations if I have concerns? How is that helpful?

Just demands money, will not answer simple questions. Aaron specifically was unprofessional. Did not offer to find someone to answer any questions. Was not asking for legal advice, just had questions.

Horrible. If you are thinking about going with them, don't! Our lawyer was even Robert Hogland himself. We barely had communication with him and when we did he was very rushed. The communication from his staff was by far the worst. Everything with our case was changing all the time. We had no idea what was going on and we were told multiple different things. I'm not even sure what we paid for because I felt like I was always the one calling and having to do the follow up work. I absolutely hated our entire experience. Save yourself and go somewhere else.

Was in a car accident and had Meshbesher. I had no idea how to handle this situation after what happened to me. Sadly I felt the lawyer I had didn't help me enough to have my back in everything and to this day years later I still have problems from the accident. It seems like it was a case where it wasn't worth the time to help me.
They only help if they can find money in your case; other than that you are dead to them.

I wish I could say great things like other reviews. Genuinely I felt hopeful with the first call. I was treated well, brought in with open arms.

Sadly it just went down from there. In the year I was with them, I was the only one making the main calls to check up on my claim. My case manager barely explained things and told me "You were the one that came to us late". I did my best to keep my cool but I felt like I was just being treated as another number. I called the service quits, and despite my letter stating I was unhappy with the service, I'm still going to be charged if I ever get approved for my disability claim.

Surely other people will have better experiences, but this was mine.

Outcome was great — disability approved. But I was never once offered a chance to meet anyone in person. Whenever I called the receptionists, who were very polite, they would never connect me to anyone I asked for. I would leave messages that were not returned sometimes for several days. I did not know who was representing me on the final hearing until 1–2 weeks before. I always felt like a number, not a person. When you hire an attorney to help you with something you can't do yourself, you want to feel connected to them. Hoglund makes you feel totally disconnected.

I am a former client, regardless of their response. Schwebel Goetz & Sieben (SGS): be prepared, this process is about money, not justice or principle.

Over two years ago, tragedy struck our family. Our 20-year-old son died, and our wrongful death claim involved the bar he visited that night. In our first meeting with our SGS attorney, we stressed that our priority wasn’t money—it was justice. He said he understood, would file charges, and would be willing to go to court. Their investigator gathered evidence supporting our claim. Then months of near silence passed. Eventually, our attorney said he would file charges; he never did. He addressed me by my deceased son’s name, twice.

Over the following months, I personally uncovered two more critical pieces of evidence. That I had to do this myself was a major red flag—this was their job. With this new evidence, our attorney set up early mediation without our approval and before filing charges. Reluctantly, we agreed. Before the mediation, he oddly showed doubt about our claim, then shook my hand and said, “You’ve done more than my investigators.”

Mediation failed. The mediator, hand-picked by SGS, was arrogant and dismissive. Our attorney repeated that the next step was to file charges. Again, it didn’t happen. Later, the insurance company made another offer based on the mediator’s suggestion. We rejected it—too low and we still wanted charges filed. But our attorney insisted we accept, otherwise find a different attorney. He gave no specifics as to why, just vague comments about the evidence.

We never requested early mediation. We were pushed into accepting a settlement we didn’t believe in. Three times we were promised that charges would be filed—they never were. The bar avoided court, responsibility, and bad publicity. For these reasons, they won, not us.

Our attorney knew the opposing counsel, had worked with him before, and likely will again. This was a business negotiation, not representation. Once you hire an SGS lawyer, you give up control. Your goals become secondary to their financial incentives. We settled, but justice wasn’t served and our son deserved better.

Cody Scharph is fantastic! Did what she was asked, no attitude, in a very timely manner, and got me a settlement in less than the time it was supposed to take. She took the time to answer my questions as the case was progressing and is extremely friendly and likeable. Number one at the top as far as I'm concerned.

Peter Riley and his secretary and assistant are awesome. I was at my very bottom and my world had turned upside down. They assisted me with my case and got me all the help to recover from my accident. I highly recommend using them.
Kojo was very pleasant to work with, and always helped me with all my questions.
I was involved in an auto accident with my wife. I can be difficult to deal with, but Shannon Carey handled it with grace and patience. In the end I received a fair settlement.

I am giving this review 4 stars because things started off a little shaky. Overall it was a great experience. Robert Hoglund explained everything to me so I knew exactly what to expect from beginning to end. I would highly recommend this firm.

I had an amazing experience with Ms. Tracy Kinney. She thoroughly reviewed over 50,000 pages of records and pointed out for the judge which page gave an example summary. She was very knowledgeable and to the point. She seemed to know the judge and exactly what she was looking for. She called me the day before the hearing and answered all my questions. After the hearing she followed up with a call to see if I had any additional questions. All in all I was very impressed!

After being denied for SSDI, I decided to hire Hoglund Law. From the very first day I knew I was dealing with professionals, answering all my questions and, during the process of waiting, keeping me informed until the day I was approved.

They answer your questions and return your calls. Attorney Beau Chinn is very nice and helpful when you are answering questions in a hearing that isn't made plain by the judge. I would recommend him and the firm he works for to anyone needing help with SSDI and SSI.

I came to Hoglund Law for help and although it took a while I was able to get the assistance I needed to finally win my case. Thanks to all of the hardworking people and my attorney Tony for making sure that I am living a better life now!
The hearing went very well. It sounded like you were prepared. The only problem for me was that I didn’t know who was handling my case at times.

A person needs to have patience as this is a long process. My end result was very satisfying and I was pleased with the lawyer who helped represent me. It was a win for me. Thank you Matt for your help.
They responded quickly and confidentially. They were honest and very generous with their time. I recommend them.
They answered the phone and returned my calls. Diligent service, answered my questions, and gave me a feeling someone was looking out for my best interest.

Andrew Kinney of hoglund law is nothing short of excellent,he got me through one of the toughest times in my life.Andrew Kinney is caring and considerate to your needs,and will be there to assist with your case from beginning to end.Andrew is knowledgeable and capable of understanding and capable of dealing with any twists or turns that may appear in short ,Andrew Kinney is the lawyer you want to represent you ,he will get the job done......

They were very good at returning your call. When you had questions they answered them within a day. Felt a little impersonal as my contact changed multiple times, I believe my atty did too. I had a few surprises at the end as those items were not discussed but nothing that mattered when I found out as they were Social security "rules". I received my determination/settlement from Social Security and did not hear from them until a month later. I think they should tell you that no matter how long you wait for your disability you are not likely to get any more than 18 months of benefits. That info is buried in your paperwork from the SS office with your settlement. They did get it done! Glad it is over.

I guess my experience with them may not be complete, but from what I have experienced so far I am frustrated. I went through them to apply for social security disability. I worked with Kong (not sure that is spelled right). He was very understanding and helped me through paperwork. Then, the denial came 3 months later, something he told me to expect. I called him. He said he would send my case to the appeals department and they would call me the next day or two. About 5 days later, I called them to see when the appeals was planning on calling. The person said they had tried to call me that morning—no message was left. I seldom answer a number I don't recognize and figure if it is important they will leave a message. The call representative said she would pass my message on to "her". It is 24 hours later and no call. It is very frustrating because there is a date on the letter and appeals have to be done within the time frame allowed. I would have given Kong a 5 star rating, but overall it is a 3. If there was a 2 1/2 I would have done that. I kind of wish I had gone to another lawyer. UPDATE: I have a new case manager now. She seems to be conscientious regarding my case. I seldom reach her on the first call, but she usually returns my call by the next day or day after.

Responses to questions are EXTREMELY slow. The paralegals are not efficient in this matter. I was frustrated in dealing with Hoglund Law regarding my Social Security disability. Lack of communication on their end. The lawyer was way better than the paralegals. The only reason I am giving 3 stars instead of 1 is because of Andrew Kinney, the lawyer who handled my case. Paralegals would receive zero stars- they were that bad at responding and communicating statuses.
owner_response: Kristi, we're glad you had a good experience with Andrew and that we could get you a fully favorable decision but if you'd like to discuss things further in private please call us at 855-513-4357

review_text: I had a five star review, but between the lack of communication and just being mocked by someone in the background of a call to either the receptionist or paralegal I have to drop a couple of stars. Maybe more soon. We'll see if anyone actually gets back to me...
owner_response: Jacob, Thank you very much for leaving us a 5 star review.

review_text: My review is mixed. I had expressed a couple times I didn't feel communication was very good at time's. But other times I would get someone that could and would answer all my questions. Depends on who your dealing with.

To start off I understand they have more than one client, the communication I had received in the beginning was terrible questions went unanswered some people might be anxious or what have you going through stuff so asking questions and … More
If you expect them to help you with Student Loan Discharge, you thought wrong. They just want money from you and want you do all the work.
owner_response: Ms. Owens, I'm sorry but I cannot discuss any private matters on a public forum. Unfortunately, there is no bankruptcy process to address student loans but please call us at 855-649-1142 so we can discuss this further.

review_text: It's hard to explain in five years ago I used them I just needed paperwork verified
Under new management so things should change
owner_response: Christina, please contact us at 855-513-4357 so we may discuss any concerns you have privately.

review_text: Does not consider taking those with less visible disabilities. If your disability isn't obvious, not matter how much proof you have, don't bother.
I agree with others. If your disability isn't visible, than don't waste your time.
My attorney today did a excellent job
Mr. Harris handled my case with expertise and genuine care. Always available, always prepared, always honest. The result was outstanding. I've already referred two friends.
Outstanding experience from start to finish with this firm. Clear communication, transparent billing, and a fantastic outcome. This is what great legal representation looks like.
Ms. Chen is exceptional. From the first consultation to the final resolution, I felt fully supported and informed. The outcome exceeded my expectations. Highly recommended.
I can't say enough good things about their team. Professional, compassionate, and incredibly effective. They turned a terrifying situation into a manageable one. 10/10.
Five stars without hesitation. Mr. Rivera is everything you want in an attorney — smart, strategic, and deeply human. Made me feel like my case actually mattered.
Outstanding experience from start to finish with their team. Clear communication, transparent billing, and a fantastic outcome. This is what great legal representation looks like.
